issue_title: Babel Transformer tries to load @babel/core relative to runtime
issue_body:
# 🐛 bug report

The babel transformer tries to require @babel/core from node_modules/@parcel/runtime-browser-hmr/lib  (“the users babel-core”), but there is no babel-core here. Then Yarn 2 throws

Note: this only fails in workspaces

A regression caused by https://github.com/parcel-bundler/parcel/pull/4526

## 😯 Current Behavior

```
Error: Cannot find module '@babel/core' from '.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/@parcel-runtime-browser-hmr-npm-2.0.0-nightly.290-56e0fefb14-3.zip/node_modules/@parcel/runtime-browser-hmr/lib'
    at .../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:115:35
    at processDirs (.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:268:39)
    at loadNodeModules (.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:293:9)
    at init (.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:104:16)
    at .../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:90:18
    at maybeRealpath (.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:41:9)
    at resolve (.../berry-repro/.yarn/cache/resolve-patch-c66c1ab009-3.zip/node_modules/resolve/lib/async.js:84:5)
    at .../berry-repro/.yarn/unplugged/@parcel-utils-npm-2.0.0-nightly.290-51f06aaf7f/node_modules/@parcel/utils/lib/promisify.js:6:13
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at .../berry-repro/.yarn/unplugged/@parcel-utils-npm-2.0.0-nightly.290-51f06aaf7f/node_modules/@parcel/utils/lib/promisify.js:5:12
```

Or
`console: (node:75178) [MODULE_NOT_FOUND] Error: @parcel/runtime-browser-hmr tried to access @babel/core, but it isn't declared in its dependencies; this makes the require call ambiguous and unsound.
`

## 💁 Possible Solution

- Do we need another flag for the babel transformer to know if it should use the bundled babel/core?
- The alternative would be requiring all runtimes to have babel/core installed


## 💻 Code Sample

```sh
yarn set version berry
echo '{"name": "test2", "workspaces": ["parcel"],"private": true}' > package.json
mkdir parcel
cd parcel
yarn init
yarn add -D parcel@nightly
touch index.js
yarn parcel index.js --no-autoinstall
```

## 🌍 Your Environment

| Software         | Version(s) |
| ---------------- | ---------- |
| Parcel           | v2
| Yarn | 2
pr_title: Reduce size of default Parcel install
pr_body:
This reduces the size of `node_modules` when installing Parcel. Rather than installing all plugins included in the default config, we only install a small subset of the most commonly used plugins (basically the JS pipeline). Other plugins are listed in the default config and auto installed into the user's project along with their peer dependencies on demand. This means that you only get the plugins you actually use in your project. The downside is a few more entries in your project's package.json in these cases.

This is implemented by resolving plugins relative to the user's project rather than the config when the config is in `node_modules` and the plugin is not listed in the `dependencies` of the config's package.json. The versions must still be listed in `parcelDependencies` in the config's package.json, which tells parcel which version range of the plugin to auto install into the user's project. The package manager will also verify that a compatible version is installed when loading the plugin.

This removes most of the `options.packageManager.require` calls from plugins. Instead, the plugin dependencies are listed either as `dependencies` or `peerDependencies`, based on whether the user will also likely need to access the same dependency. For example, `@babel/core` and `typescript` are peer dependencies, but `js-yaml` is a normal dependency. For peer dependencies, two entries will be auto installed into the project's package.json.

Closes #4729, T-630
